page: Microsoft Azure E32-8as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz, configured with 4 physical cores and 8 vCPUs via Microsoft Hyper-V hyperthreading. The instance features a massive memory allocation of 256.0 GB RAM, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

In benchmark evaluations, the AMD EPYC processor delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by stress-ng single-core CPU tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression, all scoring in the top 10%. Multi-core and multi-threaded operations, including stress-ng multi-core performance and bzip3 decompression, fall into the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks also register average performance, alongside standard database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput.

The high memory density relative to the vCPU count makes this server highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. However, a key tradeoff is the lower multi-core performance relative to the vast memory pool, as well as weak prompt processing speeds for medium-sized LLM inference models. This profile is designed for large in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise applications with high memory footprints, and memory-intensive data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-8as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Easv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It features 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores and a substantial 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. While the server lacks local storage and GPUs, it supports Premium Storage for external disk configurations. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database throughput perform in the average tier. This hardware profile offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand extensive parallel processing. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-heavy workloads.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring top-tier single-core AMD EPYC performance and 256 GB of RAM for memory-bound enterprise workloads.

Features 256.0 GB of RAM with 8 vCPUs for high memory density
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Lacks local storage and GPUs, but supports Premium Storage
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-heavy workloads
